arts.ttl: TNS star eyes Man City repeat as Welsh hopefuls make Conference League history
arts.sum: The New Saints have become the first ever side from the League of Wales to make the group stage or league phase of a major UEFA competition and begin with a huge match

arts.ttl: The Swansea City talent now facing Fiorentina on 'biggest night' for him and Welsh football
arts.sum: The midfielder was once one of Swansea City's most promising prospects - and is now about to face some of Europe's biggest names

arts.ttl: Meet the Welsh minnows with 179 fans dreaming of taking down giants Fiorentina in the Conference League - before flying home from Tuscany on Ryanair
arts.sum: JOE BERNSTEIN: The story of TNS, hailing from the village of Llansantffraid, meeting one of Serie A's aristocrats in the Conference League is a sporting tale for the ages.

arts.ttl: Conference League | Fiorentina v TNS: Match Preview
arts.sum: The Stadio Artemio Franchi in Florence will play host to a historic moment in Welsh football on Thursday evening, as The New Saints become the first Cymru Premier club to participate in a UEFA group stage competition. Their opponents, Fiorentina, are no strangers to this level, having reached the Europa Conference League final in each of the past two seasons. After a summer of change that saw Vincenzo Italiano depart and Raffaele Palladino take the reins, Fiorentina have made a stuttering start to their Serie A campaign. arts.ttl: Fiorentina’s Palladino: “We’re building a group and a family”
arts.sum: Fiorentina manager Raffaele Palladino addressed the media in a press conference ahead of the team’s Conference League debut against The New Saints. Following a draw against Empoli in the league, Palladino expressed his thoughts on the upcoming match and the team’s progress. “We’re missing a few points, but that’s precisely why we want to put in a great performance tomorrow,” Palladino stated. “Against Empoli, we were solid, although we lacked a bit in the offensive phase. Tomorrow, we want to get back to smiling. Winning helps to win, and we’re trying to create this mentality. arts.ttl: Danny Redmond spurred on by shock wins as TNS bid to surprise Fiorentina
arts.sum: The 33-year-old’s father Steve played nearly 600 league games for Manchester City, Oldham and Bury.
